description.en-US: Aquinas's commentary on the Psalms — a reportatio from his Neapolitan lectures shortly before the December 1273 mystical experience that ended his writing. Covers Psalms 1–54 (Vulgate).
description.la: Expositio Thomae in Psalmos — reportatio ex lectionibus Neapolitanis paulo ante experientiam mysticam Decembri 1273 quae scriptionem eius finivit. Psalmos 1–54 (Vulgatae) tractat.

sources.description: English translation by Stephen Loughlin (DeSales University) and Hugh McDonald, revised by the Aquinas Institute. Mirrored from aquinas.cc.
Latin text from the Leonine / Marietti editions (public domain), mirrored from aquinas.cc.

title.en-US: Prologue — Prologue
Psalm 1 — Blessed is the man
Psalm 2 — Why have the gentiles raged?
Psalm 3 — Why, O Lord are they multiplied?
Psalm 4 — When I called upon him
Psalm 5 — Give ear, O Lord, to my words
Psalm 6 — O Lord, rebuke me not
Psalm 7 — In you have I put my trust
Psalm 8 — O Lord our Lord, how wonderful is your name
Psalm 9 — I will confess to you, O Lord
Psalm 10 — In the Lord I put my trust
Psalm 11 — Save me, O Lord, for there is now no saint
Psalm 12 — How long, O Lord, will you forget me?
Psalm 13 — The fool has said in his heart, “There is no God”
Psalm 14 — Lord, who shall dwell in your tabernacle?
Psalm 15 — Preserve me, O Lord, for I have hoped in you
Psalm 16 — Hear, O Lord, my justice
Psalm 17 — I will love you, O Lord, my strength
Psalm 18 — The heavens show forth the glory of God
Psalm 19 — May the Lord hear you in the day of tribulation
Psalm 20 — In your strength, O Lord, the king shall joy
Psalm 21 — O God, my God, look upon me
Psalm 22 — The Lord rules me
Psalm 23 — The earth is the Lord’s
Psalm 24 — To you, O Lord, have I lifted up my soul
Psalm 25 — Judge me, O Lord, for I have walked
Psalm 26 — The Lord is my light and my salvation
Psalm 27 — Unto you will I cry, O Lord
Psalm 28 — Bring to the Lord, O you children of God
Psalm 29 — I will extol you, O Lord
Psalm 30 — In you, O Lord, have I hoped
Psalm 31 — Blessed are they whose iniquities are forgiven
Psalm 32 — Rejoice in the Lord, O you just
Psalm 33 — I will bless the Lord at all times
Psalm 34 — Judge, O Lord, those who wrong me
Psalm 35 — The unjust has said within himself
Psalm 36 — Be not quick to imitate evildoers
Psalm 37 — Rebuke me not, O Lord
Psalm 38 — I will take heed to my ways
Psalm 39 — With expectation I have waited
Psalm 40 — Blessed is he who understands
Psalm 41 — As the hart longs for the fountains
Psalm 42 — Judge me, O God, and distinguish my cause
Psalm 43 — We have heard, O God, with our ears
Psalm 44 — My heart has uttered a good word
Psalm 45 — God is our refuge and strength
Psalm 46 — O clap your hands, all you nations
Psalm 47 — Great is the Lord and exceedingly to be praised
Psalm 48 — Hear these things, all you nations
Psalm 49 — The God of gods, the Lord, has spoken
Psalm 50 — Have mercy on me, O God
Psalm 51 — Why do you glory in malice?
Psalm 52 — The fool said in his heart, “There is no God”
Psalm 53 — Save me, O God, by your name
Psalm 54 — Hear, O God, my prayer
